tool_name: Welded T-Joint & Angle Bracket — Weld Group Moment, Shear & Torsion Capacity

inputs.name: Weld Group Pattern
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Applied Shear Force (V_Ed)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 376:2011 §5 — Class 1 force transducer: ±1% of applied force

inputs.name: Load Eccentricity from Weld Group Centroid (e)
inputs.note: Bracket with standoff distance: e = horizontal distance from load to weld group centroid

inputs.name: Filler Metal Classification Strength (F_EXX)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: a_throat = weld_leg_mm * 0.707; const patterns = {three_side: {A: (2*plate_height_mm + plate_width_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12 + plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2))*a_throat/1e6}, four_side: {A: 2*(plate_width_mm+plate_height_mm)*a_throat, Iy: (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm/2,2) + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12)*a_throat/1e6}}; const wp = patterns['four_side']; Aw_mm2 = 2*(plate_width_mm + plate_height_mm) * a_throat; Iy_mm4 = (2*plate_width_mm*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,2)/4 + 2*Math.pow(plate_height_mm,3)/12) * a_throat  // [mm², mm⁴] — Weld Group Throat Area & Properties — Ref: Weld group elastic properties — Blodgett 'Design of Welded Structures' §7.4; AISC DG16 §3.2
f_w_allow_mpa = 0.75 * 0.6 * filler_fexx_mpa  // [MPa] — Allowable Weld Shear Stress (f_w,allow) — Ref: AWS D1.1-2020 §2.4.1 / AISC 360-22 §J2.4 — φ·f_w = 0.75 × 0.6·F_EXX on effective throat area
fv_mpa = V_Ed_kn * 1000 / Aw_mm2  // [MPa] — Direct Shear Stress in Weld (f_v) — Ref: f_v = V / A_w — direct shear on weld throat

M_total_Nmm = (M_Ed_knm + V_Ed_kn * eccentricity_mm / 1000) * 1e6; fb_mpa = M_total_Nmm * (plate_height_mm / 2) / Iy_mm4  // [MPa] — Bending Stress in Weld from In-Plane Moment (f_b) — Ref: f_b = M × c / I_y; c = d/2 (extreme fibre distance) — elastic weld group analysis per Blodgett §7.4

f_resultant_mpa = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(fv_mpa, 2) + Math.pow(fb_mpa, 2)); UC_weld = f_resultant_mpa / f_w_allow_mpa  // [MPa, dimensionless] — Resultant Weld Stress & Utilisation (Vector Sum) — Ref: Vector sum: f_res = √(f_v² + f_b²); UC = f_res / f_allow — elastic vector method per AISC DG16
a_req_mm = Math.ceil(weld_leg_mm * UC_weld / 0.707 * 0.707)  // [mm] — Required Weld Leg for Demand (a_required) — Ref: Required leg = current leg × UC_weld (if UC > 1, scale up proportionally)

standards: AISC Design Guide 16 (Flush and Extended End-Plate Moment Connections)

rules.message: Weld group overstressed — increase weld leg size, extend weld pattern to 4-sided, or reduce applied loads
Small weld leg under significant moment: moment arm is short — minimum 6 mm leg recommended for moment connections; quality control of small welds is difficult
Large eccentricity relative to plate height: torsion-like loading — verify weld group polar moment of inertia; instantaneous centre of rotation method may be required for high eccentricity

smart_warnings.message: Weld utilisation > 85%: increase leg by 2 mm increments — note that a 25% increase in leg size (e.g., 8 → 10 mm) increases capacity by 25% for the same pattern
Bending stress dominates (>3× shear): verify that weld configuration is optimised — adding weld at extreme fibres (top/bottom) is most efficient for moment resistance
Two vertical welds under moment: very low horizontal polar moment — moment creates high horizontal force with no top/bottom horizontal weld to resist it; switch to 4-sided weld pattern
Large bracket eccentricity (> 200 mm) with high shear: significant eccentric moment — verify weld group using instantaneous centre method per AISC Manual Table 8-4 for accurate capacity
Required weld leg exceeds half the plate thickness: weld will be as thick as the plate — verify plate adequate strength and consider full-penetration butt weld instead

fmea.effect: Weld group overstressed — increase weld leg size, extend weld pattern to 4-sided, or reduce applied loads
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Small weld leg under significant moment: moment arm is short — minimum 6 mm leg recommended for moment connections; quality control of small welds is difficult
Large eccentricity relative to plate height: torsion-like loading — verify weld group polar moment of inertia; instantaneous centre of rotation method may be required for high eccentricity

fmea.failureMode: Weld utilisation > 85%: increase leg by 2 mm increments — note that a 25% increase in leg size (e.g., 8 → 10 mm) increases capacity by 25% for the same pattern
Bending stress dominates (>3× shear): verify that weld configuration is optimised — adding weld at extreme fibres (top/bottom) is most efficient for moment resistance
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: fb_mpa > fv_mpa * 3

fmea.failureMode: Two vertical welds under moment: very low horizontal polar moment — moment creates high horizontal force with no top/bottom horizontal weld to resist it; switch to 4-sided weld pattern
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: weld_pattern === '2_vert' && M_Ed_knm > 0

fmea.failureMode: Large bracket eccentricity (> 200 mm) with high shear: significant eccentric moment — verify weld group using instantaneous centre method per AISC Manual Table 8-4 for accurate capacity
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: eccentricity_mm > 200 && V_Ed_kn > 50

fmea.failureMode: Required weld leg exceeds half the plate thickness: weld will be as thick as the plate — verify plate adequate strength and consider full-penetration butt weld instead
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: a_req_mm > plate_width_mm * 0.5 / 0.707
